points.text: Common COVID-19 symptoms are cough, fever, loss of smell and loss of taste [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00011] [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00001]
Other frequent symptoms include fatigue, headache, nasal congestion, runny nose, muscle pain, sore throat, diarrhea, and eye irritation [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_
In moderate to severe cases, breathing difficulties may develop [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00011]
Three symptom clusters: respiratory (cough, sputum, shortness of breath, fever); musculoskeletal (muscle/joint pain, headache, fatigue); digestive (abdominal pa
Loss of taste together with loss of smell occurs in up to 88% of symptomatic cases without prior ENT disorders [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00012]
Symptoms typically begin 1 to 14 days after exposure to the virus [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00001]
Symptoms vary by variant and may change over time [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00011]
Congestion, runny nose, or sore throat (common cold symptoms) [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00002]
Digestive issues such as upset stomach, vomiting, or diarrhea [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00002]
Headaches and body or muscle aches [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00002]
